title: Aster eriophorus
intro: Aster eriophorus, also known as the woolly aster, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family native to various parts of the world, including Africa and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Aster eriophorus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and fever.
taxonomy: Aster eriophorus is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Aster, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as asters or Michaelmas daisies. The species Aster eriophorus is native to parts of Asia and Europe. The classification of Aster eriophorus is also known as the composite or sunflower family due to its distinctive flower structure. This classification places Aster eriophorus in the phylum Magnoliophyta, which includes all flowering plants.
